paper_title: Reconstructing Soil Erosion Dynamics Using Paleolimnological Records

methods.description: Reconstructing soil erosion dynamics using paleolimnological records from lake sediments, focusing on sediment accumulation rates (SARs) and geochemical signatures.

steps.description: Paleolimnological records were used to reconstruct a signal of soil erosion at both the annual and centennial scale for the past 12,000 y.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch paleolimnological records for soil erosion reconstruction

steps.description: SARs from central profundal position in a lake can provide an estimation of gross changes in the rate of allochthonous inputs related to erosion.
steps.searchable_summary: process SARs to estimate allochthonous input changes

steps.description: Radiocarbon (14C) dating of lake sediments can document the rates at which sediment accumulated over the past 50,000 y.
steps.searchable_summary: perform radiocarbon dating on lake sediments

methods.description: Collection of palynological and radiometric data from lake sediment records and selection of suitable study sites.

steps.description: Palynological and radiometric data of lake sediment records were collected in May 2017 from three open source databases: the GPD, the EPD, and the APD.
steps.searchable_summary: collect palynological and radiometric data from databases

steps.description: Sites included in our analyses had to satisfy several conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: filter sites based on specific conditions

methods.description: Calibration of radiocarbon ages to calendar years and creation of age-depth models for sediment cores.

steps.description: A total of 4,581 radiocarbon ages were calibrated to calendar (±2 σ) years before present using the CLAM 2.2 package in R software.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate radiocarbon ages using CLAM 2.2 in R

steps.description: For each core, 14C dates were processed semiautomatically to obtain 632 age-depth models after 1,000 iterations for each profile.
steps.searchable_summary: create age-depth models for sediment cores

methods.method_name: Holocene Land Cover and Climate Data Analysis
methods.description: Analysis of land cover changes and climate data over the Holocene using pollen records and climate simulations.

steps.description: Climate time series for the past 8,000 y were collected from the transient Holocene simulation with MPI-ESM-1.2 LR.
steps.searchable_summary: collect climate time series from MPI-ESM-1.2 LR simulation

steps.description: Time series were resampled at 50 and 10 y using piecewise cubic Hermitean interpolation polynomials (Pchip-R).

methods.description: Investigation of the contribution of lake productivity to temporal variations of SARs using multiple proxies.

steps.description: Quantified the proportion of variation in the annual SAR measurements for each varved record that could be explained by the terrigenous vs. lacustrine productivity signals.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze SAR variation using terrigenous and lacustrine signals

methods.description: Use of Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) to analyze the impacts of climate and land cover drivers on erosion dynamics.

steps.description: We ran 632 models using the lake SAR time series as the response variable, the normalized AP percentage, precipitation, and air temperatures as fixed effect explanatory variables.
steps.searchable_summary: run GAMs with SAR time series and climate variables

steps.description: Confidence intervals were derived using the SEs produced by the predict.gam function in R.
steps.searchable_summary: derive confidence intervals using predict.gam in R
